title: Coaching Readiness Scan
displayTitle: Are You Actually Ready for Coaching?
tagline: A 4-minute scan that's honest about whether coaching is right for you right now.

children.text: Most coaching sites assume you're ready. This one tests it. Thirteen questions across five dimensions. No email required to see your result.

questions.prompt: I can describe in one sentence what I want to be different in my work or leadership six months from now.
The reason I'm considering coaching is mine — not primarily someone else's idea (a partner, a board, an HR programme).
If coaching worked perfectly, I would know exactly what I'd see, decide, or do differently.
In a typical week, I have at least 30 minutes of uninterrupted time to think — not work, not consume, just think.
I notice my own reactions and patterns more than I did a year ago.
After a decision goes well or badly, I usually take time to ask why before moving on.
When someone close to me names something I'm doing that isn't working, my first response is to consider whether they're right — not to defend myself.
There are at least one or two things about how I lead that I know I'm avoiding looking at.
I would rather a coach push me somewhere uncomfortable than make me feel good about where I already am.
I'm willing to do things between sessions that feel awkward at first.
My mental model of what my role requires has materially changed in the last 12 months.
I can name at least two assumptions I held two years ago about my work that I no longer hold.
I have done at least one piece of substantive learning this year that wasn't required of me.

dimensions.label: Clarity of Intent
dimensions.description: How sharply you can name what you actually want.

dimensions.label: Capacity to Reflect
dimensions.description: Whether you have the time and habit to do between-session work.

dimensions.label: Honesty with Self
dimensions.description: Whether you can hear hard feedback without armouring up.

dimensions.label: Openness to Challenge
dimensions.description: Whether you want to be confirmed or moved.

dimensions.label: Currency
dimensions.description: Whether your mental model of your role has updated to current conditions.

children.text: You're in an unusually good position to get value from coaching. The work for you is unlikely to be discovering what to change. It's executing the changes you've already half-seen.
You're ready, with one notable gap. See the read on your lowest dimension below.
You're close, but there's a foundation question to settle before coaching will pay off. Otherwise you'll spend the first three sessions doing work you could do alone, more cheaply.
Honest answer: coaching isn't the right next move for you right now. The pattern in your answers suggests something else needs to come first.

Your one gap is clarity on what you actually want. You can absolutely start coaching from here — many senior leaders have — but expect the first session or two to be spent finding the question rather than answering it. Useful to know in advance.

Your one gap is reflection bandwidth. Coaching only compounds if there's time between sessions to let things land. If your week is genuinely full, the most useful thing isn't more coaching — it's protecting 30 minutes a week, somewhere, for the work to do its work.

Your one gap is self-honesty. The hardest dimension to admit a low score on, so the fact you did is itself a good sign. The work will probably feel uncomfortable earlier than you expect — and that's the point.

Your one gap is appetite for challenge. Nothing wrong with wanting a coach who's primarily supportive — but be clear with yourself that's what you're buying. The transformative work tends to live on the other side of a few uncomfortable conversations.

Your one gap is currency. Your mental model of your role hasn't moved much, which in 2026 is itself a signal worth paying attention to. Most of the senior leaders I work with had their model forcibly updated in the last 18 months — by AI, by restructures, by their boards. If yours hasn't, it's worth asking whether the environment hasn't changed, or whether you haven't yet let it change you. Different problems, different work.

seoDescription: A 4-minute scan that's honest about whether coaching is right for you right now. Five dimensions, immediate result, no email required to see it.
